cards.title: Sleep Hygiene — Clinical Protocol

alerts.label: Sleep Architecture
alerts.text: Normal sleep: 7–9 hours adults, 8–10 teens, 9–12 children. NREM (75%) + REM (25%). Deep sleep (N3) essential for physical repair; REM for memory consolidation. Circadian rhythm governed by suprachiasmatic nucleus and melatonin (secreted 9pm–4am in darkness).

rows: Strong — jet lag, delayed sleep phase
Good — reduces cortisol, improves sleep quality

steps.title: Fixed Sleep-Wake Schedule
steps.text: Same sleep and wake time 7 days/week ±30 min. Most important single intervention — anchors circadian rhythm. Even one late night on weekends causes "social jet lag" equivalent to flying 2 time zones.

steps.title: Light Management
steps.text: Morning: bright light exposure within 30 min of waking (10 min outdoor sunlight idealises cortisol awakening response). Evening: no blue-light screens after 8pm OR blue-light blocking glasses. Bedroom: completely dark (blackout curtains) — even small light reduces melatonin 50%.

steps.title: Temperature
steps.text: Bedroom temperature 18–20°C optimal for sleep onset. Body temperature must drop 1–2°C to initiate sleep. Warm bath/shower 1–2 hours before bed paradoxically improves sleep by accelerating body cooling afterwards.

steps.title: Stimulus Control
steps.text: Bed only for sleep and sex — remove TV, phone, work from bedroom. If awake >20 min, get up and do quiet activity in dim light until sleepy. Never lie awake in bed — conditions the brain to associate bed with wakefulness.

steps.title: Sleep Restriction Therapy
steps.text: For chronic insomnia: restrict time in bed to actual sleep time (minimum 5.5 hours). Gradually extend by 15 min when sleep efficiency >85%. Most effective non-pharmacological insomnia treatment (CBT-I).

cards._legacyHtml:

Naturopathic Sleep AidsDoseEvidence
Melatonin0.5–3mg 30 min before bedStrong — jet lag, delayed sleep phase
Ashwagandha (KSM-66)300mg BDGood — reduces cortisol, improves sleep quality
Valerian root300–600mg at bedtimeModerate — reduces sleep latency
Magnesium glycinate300–400mg at bedtimeGood — especially in deficiency
L-theanine200mg at bedtimeGood — promotes relaxation without sedation
Chamomile tea2 cups eveningMild — apigenin binds GABA receptors

cards.title: Stress Management — Evidence-Based Protocols

rows: Prefrontal cortex thickening, amygdala shrinkage, cortisol ↓

pearls.label: 🟡 HPA Axis Reset Protocol
pearls.text: For burnout/adrenal fatigue: (1) Sleep 8–9hrs — prioritise above all. (2) Remove all stimulants (caffeine, sugar) for 4 weeks. (3) Ashwagandha KSM-66 300mg BD. (4) Vitamin C 1g BD (adrenal support). (5) Magnesium 400mg at night. (6) B-complex with B5 (pantothenic acid) — cofactor for cortisol synthesis. (7) Reduce exercise intensity — use yoga/walking instead of HIIT. Allow 3–6 months for full recovery.

🟡 HPA Axis Reset Protocol
For burnout/adrenal fatigue: (1) Sleep 8–9hrs — prioritise above all. (2) Remove all stimulants (caffeine, sugar) for 4 weeks. (3) Ashwagandha KSM-66 300mg BD. (4) Vitamin C 1g BD (adrenal support). (5) Magnesium 400mg at night. (6) B-complex with B5 (pantothenic acid) — cofactor for cortisol synthesis. (7) Reduce exercise intensity — use yoga/walking instead of HIIT. Allow 3–6 months for full recovery.

cards.title: Exercise Prescription — Naturopathic Approach

pearls.label: 🟡 10,000 Steps Myth
pearls.text: The 10,000 steps/day goal was a 1965 Japanese marketing campaign (not science). Current evidence: benefits plateau at 7,000–8,000 steps/day for mortality reduction. More important is breaking prolonged sitting — even 2 min of walking every 30 min sitting reduces blood glucose 30% and mortality risk significantly. Prescribe: "move every 30 minutes" rather than step count alone.

cards._legacyHtml:
TypeFrequencyDurationKey BenefitBest For
Aerobic (moderate)5x/week30 minCVD, metabolic, moodHTN, T2DM, depression, obesity
Resistance training2–3x/week45 minInsulin sensitivity, bone density, metabolismT2DM, osteoporosis, sarcopenia
HIIT2–3x/week20 minVO2max, metabolic, time-efficientFit patients, time-poor, NAFLD
YogaDaily45–60 minFlexibility, stress, HRV, painChronic pain, anxiety, arthritis
WalkingDaily30–60 minJoint-friendly, accessible, cumulative benefitElderly, beginners, arthritis
Swimming3–4x/week30 minNon-weight-bearing, cardiovascularObesity, arthritis, back pain
🟡 10,000 Steps Myth
The 10,000 steps/day goal was a 1965 Japanese marketing campaign (not science). Current evidence: benefits plateau at 7,000–8,000 steps/day for mortality reduction. More important is breaking prolonged sitting — even 2 min of walking every 30 min sitting reduces blood glucose 30% and mortality risk significantly. Prescribe: "move every 30 minutes" rather than step count alone.

cards.title: Smoking Cessation — BNYS Protocol

alerts.label: Clinical Impact
alerts.text: Smoking causes 25+ diseases. Cessation at any age improves health — within 20 min BP normalises; 24hrs carbon monoxide cleared; 1 year heart disease risk halved; 10 years lung cancer risk halved; 15 years CVD risk same as non-smoker.

steps.title: 5 As Framework
steps.text: Ask (screen every patient), Advise (clear personalised advice), Assess (readiness to quit — precontemplation/contemplation/preparation/action), Assist (set quit date + support), Arrange (follow-up at 1 week, 1 month, 3 months).

steps.title: Naturopathic Support
steps.text: Lobelia inflata (lobeline — nicotinic receptor partial agonist) 500mg TDS during quit period. Oat straw (Avena sativa) — reduces nicotine craving. Valerian — manages anxiety and irritability during withdrawal.

steps.title: Acupressure Points
steps.text: LU7 (Lieque) — lung meridian, reduces craving. Tim Mee point — between LI5 and LU7, specific anti-smoking point. Ear point Shen Men — anxiety and craving. Apply pressure for 1 min whenever craving hits.

steps.title: Breathing Technique
steps.text: 4-7-8 breathing for craving: inhale 4 sec, hold 7 sec, exhale 8 sec. Repeat 4 cycles. Works by activating PNS and giving a 19-second distraction from peak craving (which lasts only 3–5 minutes).

cards.title: Alcohol Reduction & Digital Wellbeing

alerts.label: AUDIT-C Screening (3 questions)
alerts.text: Score 0–7: Low risk. 8–15: Harmful use — brief intervention. 16–19: High risk — counselling. 20+: Dependence — specialist referral. Standard drink = 10g alcohol.

rows: ★★★★ Smartphone use >5hrs/day associated with depression, anxiety, sleep disruption
Phone-free bedroom ★. No screens 1hr before bed. Designated screen-free time. Greyscale mode (less dopamine reward).
★★★★ Cortisol ↓ 12%, NK cells ↑ 40%, BP ↓ 7/4, anxiety ↓. Effect lasts 7–30 days.
2 hours in forested area, 2x/week. Mindful — all 5 senses.
★★★★ Self-esteem and mood improve within 5 minutes of green environment exercise
Prescribe outdoor exercise over indoor gym where possible
★★★★ Loneliness = 26% ↑ mortality. Equivalent to smoking 15 cigarettes/day (Holt-Lunstad)
Screen with UCLA Loneliness Scale. Prescribe: group classes, volunteering, faith communities, pet ownership.

steps.title: Brief Intervention — FRAMES
steps.text: Feedback (personalised AUDIT risk). Responsibility (their choice). Advice (clear recommendation). Menu (options for reducing). Empathy (non-judgemental). Self-efficacy (express confidence they can do it). 5 minutes — reduces drinking 15–30% at 12 months.

steps.title: Naturopathic Support
steps.text: NAC 600mg BD (replenishes glutathione). Milk thistle 200mg TDS (hepatoprotective). B vitamins — especially B1 thiamine (alcohol depletes heavily → Wernicke's). Mg 400mg/day. Kudzu root 750–1500mg/day (reduces craving — RCT evidence).

steps.title: WARNING — Dependence
steps.text: NEVER advise cold-turkey in suspected physically-dependent drinkers — withdrawal seizures and delirium tremens are POTENTIALLY FATAL. Screen with CAGE (2+ = dependence likely). If positive → medically supervised detox (chlordiazepoxide reducing protocol). Refer urgently.

cards.title: Alcohol Reduction — Clinical Protocol

alerts.label: AUDIT — Alcohol Use Disorders Identification Test
alerts.text: Score 0-7: Low risk. Score 8-15: Harmful use — brief intervention. Score 16-19: High risk — counselling. Score 20+: Possible dependence — refer specialist.

rows: At least 2 alcohol-free days per week

steps.title: Brief Intervention — FRAMES
steps.text: Feedback (personalised risk). Responsibility (change is their choice). Advice (clear recommendation). Menu (options for reduction). Empathy (non-judgemental). Self-efficacy (express confidence they can do it). 5 minutes — reduces drinking 15-30% at 12 months.

steps.title: Naturopathic Support
steps.text: NAC 600mg BD — replenishes glutathione depleted by alcohol. Milk thistle 200mg TDS — hepatoprotective. B vitamins especially B1 thiamine — depleted by alcohol (causes Wernicke's). Magnesium 400mg — heavily depleted. Kudzu root 750-1500mg/day — reduces alcohol craving (RCT evidence).

steps.title: Warning — Dependent Drinkers
steps.text: Alcohol withdrawal in physically dependent drinkers can be FATAL (seizures, delirium tremens). Identify dependence with CAGE questionnaire. Morning drinking or tremors on abstinence = refer for medically supervised detoxification. Never advise sudden cessation in suspected dependence without medical supervision.

cards.title: Nature Therapy & Social Connection — Evidence Guide

rows: Cortisol down 12%, NK cell activity up 40%, BP down 7/4 mmHg, anxiety down
Strong — multiple Japanese RCTs. Effect lasts 7-30 days post-exposure.
Self-esteem and mood improve within 5 minutes — not proportional to duration
Better health, lower psychological distress. 120 min/week is the threshold.
Cortisol recovery faster than reading. Depression and anxiety reduction in elderly. Mycobacterium vaccae in soil — natural antidepressant.
Loneliness increases mortality by 26% — equivalent to smoking 15 cigarettes/day
Social isolation raises dementia risk 60%, depression risk 50%
Strong social connections associated with 50% lower mortality over follow-up

pearls.label: 🟡 Social Prescribing
pearls.text: Now mainstream in UK NHS — GPs refer to community activities, volunteer groups, arts programmes. As a BNYS practitioner: screen for loneliness (UCLA Loneliness Scale). Prescribe: group yoga or meditation classes, community gardens, volunteer work, faith communities, pet ownership (reduces BP and loneliness), regular face-to-face contact. Video calls are better than nothing but inferior to in-person contact — oxytocin (bonding hormone) requires physical presence for maximum release.

cards._legacyHtml:
NATURE THERAPY (SHINRIN-YOKU)
InterventionDoseEffectEvidence
Forest bathing (Shinrin-yoku)2 hours in forested area 2x/weekCortisol down 12%, NK cell activity up 40%, BP down 7/4 mmHg, anxiety downStrong — multiple Japanese RCTs. Effect lasts 7-30 days post-exposure.
Green exercise (any outdoor activity)5 minutes in green environmentSelf-esteem and mood improve within 5 minutes — not proportional to durationStrong — UK meta-analysis
Blue space (coast, rivers, lakes)120 minutes/week totalBetter health, lower psychological distress. 120 min/week is the threshold.Good — White et al. 2019, 20,000 participants
Gardening / Horticultural therapy30 min active gardeningCortisol recovery faster than reading. Depression and anxiety reduction in elderly. Mycobacterium vaccae in soil — natural antidepressant.Good
SOCIAL CONNECTION — THE FORGOTTEN VITAL SIGN
FindingEvidence
Loneliness increases mortality by 26% — equivalent to smoking 15 cigarettes/dayHolt-Lunstad meta-analysis, 3.4 million participants
Social isolation raises dementia risk 60%, depression risk 50%Multiple large cohort studies
Strong social connections associated with 50% lower mortality over follow-upBerkman and Syme, Oakland study
🟡 Social Prescribing
Now mainstream in UK NHS — GPs refer to community activities, volunteer groups, arts programmes. As a BNYS practitioner: screen for loneliness (UCLA Loneliness Scale). Prescribe: group yoga or meditation classes, community gardens, volunteer work, faith communities, pet ownership (reduces BP and loneliness), regular face-to-face contact. Video calls are better than nothing but inferior to in-person contact — oxytocin (bonding hormone) requires physical presence for maximum release.
